message:
fix: explicitly shut down extension runtimes before session drop to prevent QuickJS GC assertion

When 2+ JavaScript extensions are loaded, ExtensionRegion::drop() runs
synchronously and cannot coordinate with the QuickJS runtime thread's
garbage collector. This causes a GC assertion failure (non-empty
gc_obj_list) because JS objects are still alive when JS_FreeRuntime
asserts the heap is empty.

Fix by calling shutdown().await on extension runtimes at three exit
points before the owning session/region is dropped:

- src/main.rs run_print_mode: await agent_session.extensions.shutdown()
  before returning the result
- src/main.rs run_interactive_mode: await region.shutdown() even on
  error paths (must run unconditionally to prevent the GC assertion
  from firing during error unwinding)
- src/rpc.rs run: await session.extensions.shutdown() after the main
  event loop completes, before the session guard is released

Each shutdown site includes a comment explaining why the explicit
shutdown is necessary and what failure mode it prevents.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
